svg.text: PC1-Styx: Unified E0 + E2-E7 with Individual OUTSB
Per-scanline palette update sequence during Horizontal Blanking (HBLANK)
Extends into visible area — safe for inactive palette entries
— shared by both palettes, completes within HBLANK (~45 cy)
— zeros, also completes within HBLANK (~63 cy)
— passthrough: same value rewritten, no flicker
— pre-loaded with line N+2 colors, safe in any column
Same code path, swapped data: Even registers (E2/E4/E6) = Current · Odd registers (E3/E5/E7) = Upcoming
Two palette banks (Palette 0 / Palette 1). Flip the active bank each scanline, stream new colors to the inactive one → 3 independent colors per line.
E0 sets the background color. Rewrite it with a new value on every scanline → animated horizontal color gradients.
E0–E1 = background (color 0). E2–E7 = CGA palette colors 1–3. Each color = 2 bytes: even register = Red, odd register = Green + Blue.
E2–E7 extend past HBLANK into visible area. Successfully tested — inactive entries can be written anytime without flicker.
REP OUTSB (CX=16) — CPU stays in microcode loop for all 16 bytes. E0 committed at ~55 cy.
16× individual OUTSB — E0 committed at ~45 cy, E1 at ~63 cy. Both within HBLANK. Cleaner bars.
Individual OUTSB finishes E0 earlier — no PUSH/MOV CX/POP overhead. Fewer raster bar artifacts on real hardware.
Olivetti Prodest PC1 · NEC V40 @ 8 MHz · CGA Mode 4 · Unified E0+E2-E7 Individual OUTSB — confirmed on real hardware (March 2026)
